name: Subscriptions/basic
description: Basic Subscription conformance tests for R4/R4B classic only: create, read, search by _id, update, and delete a criteria-based rest-hook Subscription. Topic/backport subscriptions require separate coverage and no R5 topic requirements apply here. This deliberately only exercises what a black-box HTTP TestScript run can assert without inbound-callback infrastructure — persistence and round-trip of criteria/channel/status fields, not actual notification delivery. Full async delivery, handshake, and heartbeat scenarios are out of scope; see the Subscriptions ADR referenced from backend/README.md.

extension.valueString: rest.resource.where(type='Subscription').interaction.where(code='create').exists() and rest.resource.where(type='Subscription').interaction.where(code='read').exists() and rest.resource.where(type='Subscription').interaction.where(code='update').exists() and rest.resource.where(type='Subscription').interaction.where(code='delete').exists() and rest.resource.where(type='Subscription').interaction.where(code='search-type').exists() and rest.resource.where(type='Subscription').updateCreate = true

resource.reason: ignixa-lab TestScript conformance check (no real delivery expected)
ignixa-lab TestScript conformance check (no real delivery expected)

assert.description: Setup PUT must return a success status (2xx)

test.name: read returns the created Subscription with criteria and channel intact
test.description: GET Subscription/{id} must return the Subscription with the criteria, channel type, and channel endpoint that were set at creation.

assert.description: status must be a non-empty code (warningOnly: some servers immediately attempt a handshake and flip status away from 'requested')

test.name: search by _id locates the created Subscription
test.description: GET Subscription?_id={id} must return a Bundle containing the created Subscription.

assert.description: Bundle must include the created Subscription

test.name: update replaces criteria, channel endpoint, and status
test.description: PUT Subscription/{id} with a fully-specified replacement body must be accepted and change the stored criteria/channel/status.

assert.description: Update must return a success status (2xx)

test.name: read after update reflects the change
test.description: GET after the PUT must return the updated criteria and channel endpoint. status=off is warningOnly since some servers manage subscription status server-side and may not honor a client-supplied status value.

assert.description: status should reflect the update to 'off' (warningOnly: status may be server-managed)

test.name: delete removes the subscription
test.description: DELETE Subscription/{id} must return a 2xx success status.

assert.description: Delete must return a 2xx success status

test.name: read after delete returns 410 or 404
test.description: GET after DELETE must return either 410 Gone when the server tracks the deleted resource or 404 Not Found when deleted resources are not tracked.

operation.description: Attempt to read the deleted Subscription

assert.description: Accepted alternative: 410 Gone when the server tracks the deleted resource
Accepted alternative: 404 Not Found when deleted resources are not tracked

operation.description: Teardown: attempt to clean up the Subscription (may already be deleted by the delete test — that is expected)
